converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Judith Ginsburg papers
Ginsburg, Judith.
14-9-3280
2 cubic feet. 2 cubic feet.
1990-2002. Manuscripts, printed materials. Files relating to Professor Ginsburg's participation in the organization of the American Philological Association's Committee on Professional Matters and to a session on Sexual Harrassment and the Classics Profession proposed for an annual meeting of the association. Collection material in English 4768719
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Andrea Hektor
Date completed:
July 2004
EAD encoding:
Andrea Hektor, July 2004
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Classics, Cornell University. Professor Ginsburg died in 2002.
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
Where possible, original folder headings have been retained. The bulk of the collection consists of materials relating to a book Ginsburg was writing, including notes and drafts of chapters.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Personal Boxes: 1-2This series contains materials relating to a book Ginsburg was writing. It includes research materials and drafts of certain chapters.
Series II. Book materials Boxes: 1-2This series contains materials relating to a book Ginsburg was writing. It includes research materials and drafts of certain chapters.
Series III. Other Box 2
Cite As:
Judith Ginsburg papers, #14-9-3280. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Files relating to Professor Ginsburg's research and professional activities. Includes files on the organization of the American Philological Association's Committee on Professional Matters and regarding a session on sexual harassment and the Classics profession proposed for an annual meeting of the association. Also, items from her memorial service, including a eulogy by Cornell professors Lynne Abel, Jennifer Whiting, and Jeffrey Rusten.
Women college teachers. Classicists. Professional ethics. Sexual harassment in education. Cornell University. Dept. of Classics American Philological Association. Committee on Professional Matters
